bids.reasoning: At the opening time point, I am targeting my maximum-utility bid since there is no opponent history or message indicating what tradeoffs they value. With no time pressure yet, it is best to start from my strongest position across all issues, especially holding firm on the high-weight issues C and D.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-max utility bid while making a small concession on my lowest-weight issue. The opponent’s only offer is all valueA, which is extremely poor for me, and their message is aggressive cheap talk rather than evidence of a real concession pattern. I will avoid repeating my exact opening by conceding slightly on issueA while holding firm on the higher-importance issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.86 because it is still very early, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has repeated the all-valueA offer and their messages are aggressive, but their actual bidding shows no concession, so I will concede only on my lowest-weight issue while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid while making another early concession on the lowest-weight issue, since we are only at t=0.06 and there is no need to concede on issueC or issueD yet. The opponent has held firm at all valueA and used aggressive language, but their offers show no real concession, so I should not overreact to the messages.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.82 because it is still very early in the negotiation, so there is no need to make major concessions on high-weight issues yet. The opponent has repeatedly offered the all-valueA bid with no numerical concession despite aggressive messages, so I trust the offer history over the rhetoric and concede only on my lowest-weight issueA for now.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.76, since it is very early at t=0.10 and the opponent has not made any substantive concession, repeatedly offering all valueA. Their messages demand “serious concessions,” but the actual offer history shows they are holding firm, so I will concede first on lower-weight issueB while protecting the high-weight issueC and issueD.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for me, around 0.72 by my weighted preferences, while making another visible concession. The opponent has mostly held at valueA on all issues with only a small move on issueC, so I am not treating the aggressive messages as binding, but I will continue conceding on lower-weight issueB while protecting the high-weight issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.70: still well above reservation and protective of my high-weight issues, but with visible concessions compared with my prior offers. The opponent has mostly held at valueA on everything with only a small move to issueC:valueB, so I am not treating the aggressive “lowball” messages as binding, but I am conceding on lower-impact issueB and slightly on high-weight issueD to encourage movement while it is still early.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong but not maximal bid, around 0.65 utility for me rather than rushing toward the opponent’s extreme position. The opponent has mostly held at valueA on issues A/B/D and only moved issueC to valueB, so I read them as preferring lower values; I concede further on issueB while protecting the high-importance issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.58: a meaningful concession from my previous bid while still protecting the high-weight issues C and D with reasonably valuable outcomes. The opponent’s actual offers show only a small move on issueC to valueB and no movement elsewhere, so despite their aggressive messages I should not fully capitulate this early at t=0.18. I will concede further on both issueC and issueD to test whether they respond to movement on the issues they complained about.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.52, which is a further concession from my last offer while still protecting the high-weight issues. At t=0.20 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s very low offers, but their repeated bids and messages indicate issueD is especially important to them, so I am conceding there from valueD to valueC while holding issueC at a strong value.

I am targeting a moderate utility concession around the mid-game while still preserving value on the high-weight issueC. The opponent has remained very firm near valueA and keeps objecting particularly to issueD, so I concede further on issueD while keeping issueA and issueB at their preferred low end to improve acceptability without collapsing my payoff.

I am targeting a utility around 0.49, which is a meaningful concession from my early high-utility offers while still protecting value on the high-weight issues. At t=0.24 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s extreme terms, but their repeated bids show they strongly prefer issueA/issueB/issueD near valueA and have only moved on issueC; I therefore concede issueA and issueB fully, keep issueC at a value they have already offered, and move issueD closer to them without giving it away entirely.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.465, conceding substantially from my high-utility positions while still preserving value on issueC. At t=0.26 there is time left, but the opponent has been very firm and has repeatedly offered issueD: valueA while recently showing movement on issueB and issueC, so I am testing a compromise that incorporates those signals without fully capitulating.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.52: a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids while still protecting value on the high-weight issueC and issueD. At t=0.28 there is pressure to move, but it is not late enough to accept the opponent’s very low-utility anchors; their offers show only limited movement, with recent signals that issueD: valueC and some movement on issueC may be feasible despite the aggressive messaging.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.55: still well above reservation, but with a visible concession on issueD while preserving strong value on high-importance issueC. The opponent’s messages are mostly pressure tactics, but their offers show small concessions on issueB and issueC, including their latest issueB: valueD, so I use that signal rather than the aggressive wording.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.54, conceding further from my prior position without dropping near the opponent’s very low-value offers. At t=0.32 there is still time, so I should not capitulate, but the opponent’s bids show some movement on issueB, issueC, and issueD, so I am combining those signals into a broader compromise while holding some value on the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a utility around the mid-0.5 range: still above my reservation and preserving value on high-weight issueC/issueD, while making some concessions on lower-weight issueA/issueB. At t=0.34 there is time pressure to move toward a viable compromise, but not enough to accept the opponent’s mostly low-value offers; their messages are aggressive cheap talk, while their bids show only limited concessions such as issueA:valueB, issueB:valueD, issueC:valueD, and issueD:valueC/D.

I targeted a utility of about 0.50 for myself, making a clear mid-game concession while still staying well above reservation value. The opponent’s messages are mostly pressure tactics, but their bids show they prefer lower values and have recently moved to issueB:valueC and issueC:valueB, so I match those signals while holding issueD at valueC rather than conceding all the way to valueA.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around the mid-game rather than conceding to the opponent’s very low offers, since t=0.38 leaves time to keep pressure while still moving toward combinations they have signaled. The opponent’s messages are aggressive but their actual bids show limited concessions, especially on issueC and occasional movement on issueA/issueB/issueD, so I am combining values they have each offered before while holding a defensible position on the high-importance issues.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game concession around utility 0.56, still well above reservation while moving meaningfully from my prior offer. The opponent has gradually improved their offers but remains far below my acceptable range, and their aggressive messages are cheap talk; I am matching their latest signals on lower-weight issueA/issueB while conceding issueD further and protecting issueC somewhat.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.54, conceding further from my prior offer while still keeping value above the opponent’s latest bid to me. The opponent’s messages are consistently aggressive cheap talk, but their actual offers show gradual movement, especially on issueC and issueD, so I align with their latest positions there while asking for a modest improvement on issueB.

I am targeting a mid-range utility slightly above the opponent’s latest offer to me while still preserving value on the higher-weight issues. At t=0.44 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their exact position, and their offers show gradual movement on issueB/issueC/issueD despite the aggressive messages, so I am matching much of their latest structure while asking for issueB at a value they have previously offered.

I targeted a mid-game utility around 0.59, still above my recent concession level while combining concessions the opponent has actually signaled across prior offers. The opponent’s language is aggressive but their bids show occasional movement on issueB, issueC, and issueD, so I am offering their preferred issueA while holding moderate values on my higher-weight issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.51: enough concession to improve acceptance chances while still protecting value on the high-weight issues. The opponent’s messages are mostly pressure tactics, but their offers show some real movement on issueC and occasional movement on issueB/issueD, so I am matching some of those signals without fully collapsing to their latest low-value issueD demand.

I am targeting a mid-game compromise around 0.57 weighted utility, conceding on low-weight issueA and moderately on issueB/issueC while preserving more value on high-weight issueD. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly for me and their messages are mostly pressure/lowball claims, so I trust the bid history more than the rhetoric. Their recent bids signal some willingness around issueB=valueB and issueD=valueD, so I am combining those with a concession on issueC to valueB.

I am targeting a moderate utility of about 0.52, which is a meaningful concession from my prior bids while still protecting value on the high-weight issueC. At t=0.52 we are in the mid-game, so I am moving toward the opponent’s latest signals by accepting their offered issueA=valueB, issueB=valueC, and issueC=valueD, while asking them to improve issueD from valueA to valueB. Their messages are aggressive cheap talk, but their offers show gradual movement on issueB and issueC, with issueD still the main sticking point.

I am targeting a moderate utility counteroffer around the low 0.6 range, since we are just past the midpoint and should concede but not collapse to the opponent’s terms yet. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat, especially by recently conceding on issueD and issueB, while their messages are repetitive pressure tactics rather than binding information. I will keep their latest favorable concession on issueD, accept a weaker issueA/issueB position, and ask for a small improvement on issueC over their last offer.

At t=0.56 I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.58, conceding further from my last offer while still protecting the higher-weight issues C and D. The opponent’s actual offers have slowly improved for me on issueA and issueC but remain very poor on issueB and issueD, so I am incorporating their recent signals on A/C while offering a compromise on D rather than dropping to their preferred extreme.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility of about 0.64 while making a concrete concession pattern based on the opponent’s actual bids rather than their repeated “lowball” messaging. The opponent’s latest offer gives me issueB: valueF and issueD: valueC, and they have previously shown willingness on issueC around valueD/valueE, so I preserve value on high-weight issueC while conceding low-weight issueA fully.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.63, conceding substantially from my early positions while still improving on the opponent’s latest offer for me. At t=0.60 there is real but not final deadline pressure, so I am moving close to their recent signals—especially accepting issueA:valueB, issueB:valueA, and issueD:valueE—while asking for a small improvement on my high-weight issueC. The opponent’s messages are consistently aggressive, but the actual offers show gradual concessions, with issueD:valueE recently appearing as acceptable to them.

I am targeting a moderate concession around 0.59 utility, since we are past the midpoint but not yet at the deadline. The opponent’s actual bids show some movement on issueB and issueA, while they continue pressing hard on issueD and issueC; I am conceding substantially on the high-weight issues but not all the way to their latest demand. Their repeated “lowball” messages are cheap talk, so I rely mainly on the offer pattern.

At t=0.64 I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.58 rather than holding near my earlier high-utility positions, since the opponent has slowly improved their offers but remains resistant. Their latest bid gives me strong value on issueC and acceptable value on issueB while demanding issueA and issueD; I will accept their issueA concession and keep their strong issueC move, but ask for a small improvement on issueD instead of taking their worst value there.

At t=0.66 I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.68 rather than holding out for top values, since the opponent’s offers have improved and the deadline is getting closer. The opponent’s latest bid already gives me acceptable value on issueB and issueD, so I keep those while asking for a small improvement on the high-weight issueC; I treat their repeated “lowball” messages as pressure tactics and rely mainly on the offer trend.

At t=0.68 I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.545, conceding substantially from my earlier high-utility demands while still preserving value on the important issueC/issueD dimensions. The opponent’s messages are repetitive pressure tactics, but their actual offers show gradual movement into valueB/valueC/valueD ranges; their latest bid is usable as a baseline, so I only improve issueD one step for myself while keeping the rest aligned with their signaled terms.
